category_title: Students with aggressive behaviors and impulse control difficulties who connect through animal-related interests
minimal_context: Dominic is playing with blocks during free choice time and needs to transition to cleanup.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Free choice time is over, it is time to clean up the blocks.

STUDENT: (Takes materials away from another child) No.

TUTOR: We need to start cleaning up now.

STUDENT: (Hits the other child) I am playing with these.

TUTOR: Please put the blocks away.

STUDENT: (Becomes frustrated and throws the blocks) No clean up!

options.A: Adhere to established rules and penalties for behavior, starting with a warning, followed by a timeout, and then increasingly serious disciplinary measures.
options.B: Implement an in-class reward system where he earns an animal sticker for his chart whenever he successfully completes the transition.
options.C: Provide one-on-one time with an intervention specialist to read animal books and support emerging emotional connections.
options.D: Model appropriate transition behaviors using puppets during free choice time to demonstrate how to clean up.
